question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the getSupply() query does not filter out entities where deleted=true, so that deleted accounts' stale balances are permanently included in the unreleased supply sum, causing the reported releasedSupply() to reflect a historically incorrect and manipulated HBAR circulation figure?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the balance field on an entity row in the unreleased supply range holds a stale pre-deletion value, causing getSupply() to sum it into unreleased_supply and return a consensus_timestamp that does not correspond to any real Hashgraph consensus event, thereby misrepresenting network history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getSupply() uses cast(coalesce(max(e.balance_timestamp), 0) as bigint) as consensus_timestamp, where balance_timestamp is the last balance update time rather than a true Hashgraph consensus timestamp, causing the API to report a fabricated consensus_timestamp that misrepresents when the supply state was actually agreed upon by the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that getSupply() computes unreleased_supply as cast(coalesce(sum(e.balance), 0) as bigint), and if any entity in the range has a negative balance value stored (due to a data integrity issue), the sum could underflow, causing releasedSupply() = TOTAL_SUPPLY - unreleasedSupply to return a value exceeding TOTAL_SUPPLY and misrepresenting Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the getSupply() query to return a consensus_timestamp of 0 by ensuring no entity in the unreleased supply ranges has a balance_timestamp set, bypassing the EntityNotFoundException check in NetworkServiceImpl and returning a falsified supply response with timestamp 0 that implies the supply was valid at the genesis of the ledger?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a deleted filter in getSupply() to observe that an account deleted mid-history still contributes its last known balance to the unreleased supply, making the mirror node report a supply figure inconsistent with the actual on-chain state at any given consensus timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the getSupply() query joins entity rows using unnest(cast(string_to_array(:lowerBounds, ',') as bigint[]), cast(string_to_array(:upperBounds, ',') as bigint[])) and that if the lowerBounds or upperBounds strings contain duplicate values, the same entity's balance is summed multiple times, inflating unreleased_supply and distorting the reported Hashgraph supply history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that getSupply() does not apply any timestamp filter on the entity table, meaning it always reads the current balance regardless of what the Hashgraph ledger recorded at a specific consensus point, causing the /api/v1/network/supply endpoint (when called without a timestamp) to return a supply figure that does not correspond to any verifiable historical Hashgraph state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getSupply() uses max(e.balance_timestamp) as the consensus_timestamp, so if one entity in the range has a future-dated balance_timestamp (due to a clock skew or data injection), the returned consensus_timestamp will be in the future, making the mirror node report a supply state anchored to a non-existent future Hashgraph consensus event?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the getSupply() query sums balance across all entities in the configured ranges without verifying that each balance was recorded at the same consensus_timestamp, causing the returned unreleased_supply to be a mix of balances from different historical Hashgraph states, producing a supply figure that never existed at any single point in ledger history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findByAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the query 'select id from entity where alias = ?1 and deleted <> true' where deleted <> true evaluates to NULL (falsy) for rows with deleted=NULL, causing entities with a null deleted flag to be silently excluded from alias lookups, leading to a false 'entity not found' response that misrepresents the current Hashgraph state of that account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findByEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the query 'select id from entity where evm_address = ?1 and deleted <> true' where deleted <> true excludes NULL-deleted entities, causing a valid EVM address that maps to an entity with deleted=NULL to return empty, making the mirror node report that an address has no on-chain history when it actually does?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findByAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a zero-length byte array as the alias parameter to findByAlias(), causing the query to match any entity row where alias is an empty byte string, potentially resolving to an unintended entity ID and misrepresenting the Hashgraph account associated with that alias?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findByEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an all-zero 20-byte EVM address to findByEvmAddress(), causing the query to match a system entity or genesis account that has evm_address=0x0000...0000, and thereby obtain an entity ID that misrepresents the Hashgraph identity of the zero address?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the getSupply() query uses cast(... as bigint) for both unreleased_supply and consensus_timestamp, and if the sum of balances across all unreleased supply accounts exceeds Long.MAX_VALUE (9,223,372,036,854,775,807), the cast will overflow and return a negative unreleased_supply, causing releasedSupply() to return a value greater than TOTAL_SUPPLY and misrepresenting the Hashgraph supply history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the getSupply() query does not validate that the returned consensus_timestamp corresponds to an actual block or transaction in the Hashgraph ledger, allowing a stale or injected balance_timestamp to be surfaced as the authoritative consensus_timestamp in the /api/v1/network/supply response, misleading consumers about the ledger's historical state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the getSupply() query uses e.balance without any NOT NULL check, so if an entity in the unreleased supply range has balance=NULL, coalesce(sum(e.balance), 0) silently treats it as 0, causing the unreleased supply to be understated and the releasedSupply() to be overstated relative to the true Hashgraph ledger state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the getSupply() query's join condition 'on e.id between ranges.min_val and ranges.max_val' is an inclusive range, and if the lowerBounds and upperBounds strings are misconfigured to overlap (e.g., range 2-100 and 50-200), entities in the overlap are counted once per matching range, inflating unreleased_supply and distorting the reported Hashgraph supply?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findByAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that findByAlias() returns only the entity id (not the full entity), and if two non-deleted entities share the same alias value in the database (due to a data integrity gap), the query returns the first matching row non-deterministically, causing the mirror node to resolve the alias to a different entity than what the Hashgraph ledger recorded?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findByEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that findByEvmAddress() returns only the entity id, and if two non-deleted entities share the same evm_address (due to a reuse scenario after deletion where the deleted filter is bypassed), the query returns a non-deterministic result, causing the mirror node to map the EVM address to the wrong Hashgraph entity?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the getSupply() query's string_to_array(:lowerBounds, ',') parsing does not validate that each element is a valid bigint before casting, so if the lowerBounds string contains a non-numeric token (e.g., due to a configuration injection), the cast fails with a database error that leaks internal query structure in the error response?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the getSupply() query returns consensus_timestamp as max(e.balance_timestamp), and since balance_timestamp is updated by the importer independently of consensus, a delayed or out-of-order import could cause the returned consensus_timestamp to be earlier than the actual latest Hashgraph consensus event, making the supply appear anchored to an older historical state than it actually is?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the getSupply() query does not use a snapshot-isolation transaction, so if the entity table is being updated concurrently by the importer while getSupply() executes, the query may read a mix of pre- and post-update balance values, producing a supply figure that corresponds to no single consistent Hashgraph state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findByAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a byte array alias that matches a system account (e.g., account 0.0.2) in the entity table, causing findByAlias() to return the ID of a treasury or system account and allowing downstream code to treat user-controlled input as a privileged Hashgraph entity?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findByEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a 20-byte EVM address that collides with the EVM address of a system contract (e.g., 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000167), causing findByEvmAddress() to return the system contract's entity ID and allowing the caller to impersonate a privileged Hashgraph entity in subsequent operations?
